TSLA: LMND Stock Rises Pre-Market: Morgan Stanley Backs Its Early Bet On Autonomous Vehicle Insurance. Morgan Stanley has upgraded Lemonade Inc. (LMND) to an ‘Overweight’ rating and increased its price target to $85, citing the insurer's partnership with Tesla for autonomous vehicle insurance. Lemonade's early entry into this market, including offering a 50% discount for Tesla's Full Self-Driving users, positions it ahead of competitors and is expected to significantly improve its long-term earnings. The firm noted that Lemonade's pricing remains disciplined despite the aggressive discounts.

US government confirms Tesla and LG Energy Solution's $4.3 billion battery deal. The U.S. government has confirmed a $4.3 billion supply agreement between Tesla and South Korea's LG Energy Solution to build a lithium iron phosphate (LFP) prismatic battery cell manufacturing facility in Lansing, Michigan. This facility is expected to launch production in 2027 and will supply cells for Tesla's Megapack 3 energy storage systems, aiming to create a domestic battery supply chain and reduce reliance on Chinese imports. This agreement was highlighted as part of a broader statement on deals from the Indo-Pacific Energy Security Summit.

Tesla US Sales Sank 26% In January, But 5 Automakers Saw Electric Vehicle Growth. In January, EV registrations in the U.S. dropped significantly by 41% year-over-year following the expiration of the federal EV tax credit in September 2025. Tesla's US sales declined by 26%, attributed to the tax credit's removal. Despite the overall market downturn, several automakers including Cadillac, Toyota Motors, Lucid Group, Maserati, and Lexus reported year-over-year sales growth, mainly due to lower volumes or new model introductions.

Is Tesla Expanding Beyond EVs Into Energy Supply in UK?. Tesla has received approval from the UK energy regulator Ofgem to directly supply electricity to households and businesses across Great Britain through its Tesla Energy Ventures subsidiary. This move signifies Tesla's expansion beyond electric vehicles into the energy sector, mirroring its Texas retail electricity model which focuses on providing low-cost, sustainable electricity. Despite a decline in UK vehicle sales and some public opposition, the license allows Tesla to further its energy initiatives, following previous approvals for electricity generation and large-scale battery storage projects.

Tesla Upgraded, Super Micro Computer Downgraded: Updated Rankings on Top Blue-Chip Stocks. Louis Navellier, editor of Growth Investor, has released updated Stock Grader recommendations for 114 blue-chip stocks based on institutional buying pressure and fundamental health. The rankings include numerous upgrades and downgrades, with Tesla, Inc. moving from Neutral to Strong and Super Micro Computer, Inc. being downgraded from Neutral to Weak. Investors are encouraged to review the list to assess their current holdings and potential profit opportunities.

Tesla range claims are bogus, owner says in suit. He makes barely half.. A Texas Tesla owner has filed a lawsuit against the automaker, alleging fraud over inaccurate range claims for his 2022 Model X. Despite purchasing a "long-range" option, the owner states his vehicle achieves barely half of the advertised 300+ miles on a full charge. The suit highlights broader concerns about Tesla's exaggerated range estimates and its alleged dismissive approach to customer complaints and service issues regarding battery performance.
TGT: Target (NYSE: TGT) CAO covers tax obligations through share withholding. Target Corporation's Chief Accounting Officer, Matthew A. Liegel, settled tax obligations by withholding shares upon the vesting of restricted stock units. On two separate occasions in March 2026, a total of 617 shares were withheld, valued at $116.55 and $116.73 per share, respectively, under the company's 2020 Long-Term Incentive Plan. Following these transactions, Liegel directly holds 14,196 Target shares.

Target offers Circle loyalty members three-day spring savings event. Target is hosting a three-day "Target Circle Days" savings event from March 25-27, 2026, offering discounts of up to 50% on various items for its free Circle loyalty program members. Members of the paid Target Circle 360 tier will get early access on March 24. This event, which includes daily deals and incentives for new members, aims to provide value during the spring season and competes with Amazon's annual Big Spring Sale.

Perry: Why I no longer shop at Target — and am not going back (yet). The author explains why his family has stopped shopping at Target, citing concerns beyond the DEI boycott. He points to Target's decision to pull Pride merchandise and its silence regarding the detention of employees by ICE as reasons for their change in shopping habits. A marketing professor is quoted, stating that while customers can be lost due to "moral violations," winning them back is very expensive and requires correctly diagnosing the underlying problem.
SBUX: Starbucks Corporation $SBUX Shares Sold by Focus Partners Wealth. Focus Partners Wealth reduced its holdings in Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) by 21.2% in the third quarter, now owning 729,741 shares valued at $61,734,000. Other institutions like Brighton Jones LLC and Schnieders Capital Management LLC increased their stakes in the coffee giant. Analysts currently rate SBUX as a "Moderate Buy" with an average target price of $104.22, despite some recent data breach concerns and an EPS miss last quarter.

CSM Advisors LLC Sells 50,685 Shares of Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. CSM Advisors LLC significantly reduced its stake in Starbucks Corporation ($SBUX) by selling 50,685 shares, bringing their total holdings to 19,650 shares valued at $1,661,000. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Starbucks, with some increasing and others acquiring new stakes. Starbucks' financial performance shows a market capitalization of $111.45 billion and recent earnings that missed analyst expectations, contributing to a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $104.22.

Assessing Starbucks (SBUX) Valuation After Mixed Share Performance And Slower International Growth Concerns. Starbucks (SBUX) stock has experienced mixed performance recently, with short-term dips contrasting with medium-term gains, while revenue and net income have shown growth. Despite this, a common analyst narrative suggests the stock is overvalued at $97.82, with a fair value estimated at $67.08, due to concerns over international growth in India and China. The article advises investors to assess the numbers themselves, considering both optimistic forecasts for earnings and potential risks from cost pressures or shifting consumer demand.

Nasdaq 100 Stock Analysis: Starbucks vs. MercadoLibre & Costco Performance - News and Statistics. An analysis of the Nasdaq 100 index reveals contrasting performances among key companies. Starbucks is facing challenges with declining operating margins and slower earnings per share growth despite revenue increases. In contrast, MercadoLibre and Costco are exhibiting strength, with MercadoLibre showing strong revenue per user growth and free cash flow margin, while Costco boasts consistent same-store sales growth and industry-leading return on capital.

NYC Comptroller Urges SBUX Board Accountability After Labor Conflicts (SBUX). The NYC Comptroller is urging Starbucks shareholders to push for board accountability, citing concerns over the company's handling of labor conflicts. The Comptroller's office has engaged with Starbucks since union organizing began in 2021, and notes a shift in board oversight after the CEO consolidated the roles of Chair and CEO in September 2024, leading to a perceived decline in independent engagement regarding labor issues. The report highlights recurring unfair labor practice claims and stalled collective bargaining, recommending a restoration of robust board-level oversight.
DIS: Focus Partners Wealth Purchases 64,313 Shares of The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Focus Partners Wealth increased its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 9.9% in the third quarter, acquiring an additional 64,313 shares and bringing its total holdings to 712,164 shares valued at over $81.5 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Walt Disney. The company recently reported strong Q1 2026 earnings, exceeding analyst expectations, and has received a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from Wall Street analysts with an average target price of $135.80.

Disney names ABC News Group's Debra OConnell as chairman of entertainment TV. Walt Disney has appointed Debra OConnell, previously president of the ABC News Group, as the chairman of Disney Entertainment Television. In her new role, OConnell will oversee various brands including ABC Entertainment, Disney Branded Television, and Hulu Originals, and will continue to manage ABC News and ABC-owned television stations. This appointment is part of Disney's new leadership structure to integrate its streaming, film, television, games, and digital entertainment businesses, with OConnell reporting to Dana Walden, president and chief creative officer.

Leadership Team for Expanded Disney Entertainment Segment. The Walt Disney Company announced a new leadership structure for its expanded Disney Entertainment segment, led by incoming President and Chief Creative Officer Dana Walden. This consolidation brings together streaming, film, television, and growing games businesses to enhance storytelling across platforms. Key appointments include Debra OConnell as Chairman of Disney Entertainment Television and Alan Bergman continuing as Chairman of Studios, while Joe Earley and Adam Smith become co-presidents of Direct to Consumer.

Dana Walden Sets Leadership Team at Disney Entertainment. Dana Walden has announced her new leadership team for Disney Entertainment, effective Wednesday, which includes Debra OConnell as chairman of Disney Entertainment Television and the integration of Disney's gaming business under Walden's purview. Sean Shoptaw, head of the gaming division, will now report to Walden, emphasizing a unified approach across streaming, film, television, and gaming. These changes aim to enhance collaboration and align creative engines to deliver Disney's storytelling across various platforms.

Inside the Discrimination Lawsuit Against Disney by a ‘Star Wars’ Executive. A Disney executive, Jay Ong, who oversees Marvel Games, Lucasfilm Games, and Disney and Pixar Games, is suing The Walt Disney Company for $40 million. He alleges retaliation and discrimination after his compensation was cut despite acknowledged "exceptional performance" and record profitability in his division, attributing the cuts to an HR executive attempting to "dig up dirt" on him. The lawsuit also claims a broader pattern of discrimination against individuals of Asian descent at Disney.
NKE: Final Trade: BA, C, NKE, SLB. CNBC's Melissa Lee and the 'Fast Money' traders presented their final trades of the day, focusing on the stocks BA (Boeing), C (Citigroup), NKE (Nike), and SLB (Schlumberger). The segment provides a quick roundup of expert opinions on these specific companies as the trading day concludes.
